decision: AC1 Postgres backup/restore: add scripts/pg-backup.sh + scripts/pg-restore.sh (pg_dump -Fc / pg_restore --clean --if-exists), connection resolved identically to lib/db/config.ts (DATABASE_URL else PGHOST/PGDATABASE/PGUSER, fail-closed). Compose backup = new profiled `pg-backup` service + `daax-pg-backups` volume in BOTH compose files; default `up` unaffected (profile: backup). Runbook: docs/postgres-backup-restore.md (backup, restore, restore drill, pre-deploy snapshot).
rationale: Named volume daax-pg-data already persistent in both files (verified, documented). Profiled run-once service avoids a long-running sidecar and is cron-drivable via `docker compose --profile backup run --rm pg-backup`. Atomic .partial->rename so a crashed dump never looks valid.

decision: AC2 secret rotation no-outage: DAAX_PROXY_SECRET dual-value already in main (lib/auth-trust.ts proxySecretMatches). Added DAAX_WS_TOKEN_SECRET_PREVIOUS support to lib/ws-ticket.ts verifyTicket: signature accepted against current OR previous (constant-time timingSafeEqual per candidate); mint always uses current. Wired DAAX_WS_TOKEN_SECRET_PREVIOUS env passthrough in both compose files (environment block only — not the gh-185 hardening stanza).
rationale: Previous branch runs ONLY when _PREVIOUS is set, so unset == byte-for-byte pre-rotation current-only behavior (no weakening). secret-unset check still gates on current secret. Rotation window need only exceed WS_TICKET_TTL_MS.

decision: AC3 migration rollback: all 3 existing migrations already ship working `down` (dropTable/dropColumn, reverse dependency order) — verified, none added. Extended tests/integration/pg-migrate.test.ts with an explicit up->down->up round-trip asserting schema returns (schema_meta+baseline row, built_images, releases, built_images.sbom_json column re-present).
rationale: Prior test covered up + idempotent-up + down; added the re-up to complete the round-trip evidence the down steps leave a rebuildable state.

decision: DEFERRED reconcileRoles --dry-run: NOT implemented — depends on unmerged #101 RBAC (reconcileRoles machinery). Recorded as a deferral note in docs/postgres-backup-restore.md §8 instead of stub code, to avoid colliding with gh-101's lib/auth.ts RBAC surface.
rationale: Task constraint: do not create reconcileRoles code that collides with gh-101. Doc note + this log satisfy the 'short note referencing it lands with #101' requirement without touching gh-101-owned files.

decision: Adversarial-review fixes (MERGE-WITH-NITS): (1) pg-restore.sh redacts the password from the printed DATABASE_URL target label (sed '://[^@/]*@' -> '://***@') — verified no password in stderr. pg-backup.sh already used a literal source_label (no leak). (2) pg-restore.sh adds --single-transaction so a mid-restore failure rolls back to pre-restore state (was --clean --exit-on-error, non-atomic drop-then-recreate = data-loss risk). (3) integration test adds a `down 1`/`up 1` step asserting sbom_json column drops while built_images survives — non-vacuously exercises the column down-step (down-all masked it). (4) ws-ticket test adds mint-during-rotation then drop _PREVIOUS re-verify, proving mint-uses-current non-vacuously. (5) compose pg-backup: trap rm .partial on EXIT + no-new-privileges + cap_drop:[ALL].
rationale: user:postgres intentionally NOT set — empirically the fresh root:root:0755 named volume blocks uid-999 writes; root + cap_drop ALL + no-new-privileges runs pg_dump and writes fine (verified via real compose run). Redaction + single-transaction verified live; column down-step + mint-uses-current pass against real PG.
